---
title: "Research"
---

*Developing and applying statistical methodology for clinical trials and causal inference, grounded in real‑world scientific collaboration.*


At the University of Iowa, I am building and leading interdisciplinary research partnerships through our

**[Causal Inference Collaboratory](https://iphprp.org/opportunities/faculty/collaboratories/causal-inference/)**  

and am involved in multicenter clinical trials through our

**[Clinical Trials Statistical and Data Management Center via the Data Coordinating Center and NeuroNEXT](https://neuronext.org/profile/emily-roberts-phd/)**

A central focus of my work is the evaluation and validation of **surrogate and intermediate endpoints** in clinical trials.  
More broadly, my methodological interests include:

- Clinical trial design and analysis  
- Causal inference  
- Survival and time‑to‑event analysis  
- Longitudinal and repeated‑measures data  
- Bayesian methods  
- Futility and interim monitoring  
- Biomarkers 

These methods are developed and applied in substantive collaborations across **diabetes**, **neurologic disease**, and **cancer**.

## Training and Recognition

My research training began at the University of Michigan, where I joined the **NIH Cancer Training Grant in Biostatistics** in 2016 and was later supported as a **National Science Foundation Graduate Research Fellow**.

During my doctoral training, I received the University of Michigan Department of Biostatistics *Excellence in Research Award* (Honorable Mention).  More recently, my work has been recognized with the *American Statistical Association Lifetime Data Science Paper Award*, as well as the *Junior Faculty Opportunity Award* and *New Faculty Research Award* at the University of Iowa.


## Selected Publications

Below is a selected list of peer‑reviewed publications reflecting my methodological and collaborative research agenda.  
A complete and up‑to‑date list of papers and presentations is available on my   **[Google Scholar profile](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=yh2UbZEAAAAJ&hl=en)**.

---

Sursely, A. M., Nash, S. H., Sadler, A. G., **Roberts, E. K.**, Torner, J. C., Platt, J. M. (2025)  
Exploring contextual details in suicide precipitating circumstances:  A qualitative content analysis of NVDRS narrative reports  
*Journal of Psychiatry and Brain Science*

---

Sursely, A. M., **Roberts, E. K.**, Nash, S. H., Sadler, A. G., Torner, J. C., Platt, J. M. (2025)  
Crisis circumstances in U.S. suicide deaths:  A latent class analysis of NVDRS data from 2013–2021  
*American Journal of Preventive Medicine*

---

Smith, M. J., **Roberts, E. K.**, Charlton, M. E., Oleson, J. J. (2025)  
Incorporating small‑area estimation into mediation analyses with areal datasets  
*Spatial and Spatio‑temporal Epidemiology*

---

Williams, P. A., **Roberts, E. K.**, Nguyen, Q. A., Rahmouni, K. (2025)  
Identifying unimodal finite mixture models using distribution shifting  
*Results in Engineering*

---

Tanaka, T., **Roberts, E. K.**, Platt, J., Axelrod, D. (2025)  
Longitudinal assessment of performance status in U.S. liver transplantation:  National trends and survival implications  
*Transplantation Direct*

---

**Roberts, E. K.**, Elliott, M. R., Taylor, J. M. G. (2023)  
[Surrogacy validation for time‑to‑event outcomes with illness–death frailty models](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdfdirect/10.1002/bimj.202200324)  
*Biometrical Journal*

---

**Roberts, E. K.**, Elliott, M. R., Taylor, J. M. G. (2023)  
[Solutions for surrogacy validation with longitudinal outcomes for a gene therapy](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/pdfdirect/10.1111/biom.13720)  
*Biometrics*
